description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a blocky, angular form and multiple flat and sloped surfaces. The rock has a predominantly gray coloration with subtle variations in shading, giving it a natural stone appearance. Its surface is rough and matte, with visible edges and facets that suggest it has been weathered or broken from a larger formation. The rock is composed of several interlocking segments, some of which protrude outward, creating ledges and small platforms. There are no visible markings, carvings, or artificial modifications on its surface. The overall size and mass of the rock make it suitable as a natural barrier or landscape feature in an urban park or similar setting.
description: A large, angular gray rock with rough, blocky surfaces and natural stone texture. Its irregular shape and size make it suitable as a barrier or landscape element.
description_view_0: The rock appears broad and squat, with several prominent flat faces and a large, sloped segment on the left side.
description_view_1: This view shows the rock as taller and narrower, with vertical faces and a more pronounced height.
description_view_2: The back reveals a rounded, less angular surface with fewer protrusions and a smoother overall profile.
description_view_3: From this angle, a large, flat face is visible on the lower right, and the rock appears to lean slightly with a tall, vertical segment.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
